channel.title: Daily Digest - AI/Tech/Science
channel.description: High-signal curated digest from Hacker News, arXiv, chemistry, and biotech research

item.title: Digest — Apr 03
item.description:
Hacker News

  • Show HN: Apfel – The free AI already on your Mac (284 pts)
  • Show HN: I built a frontpage for personal blogs (44 pts)
  • April 2026 TLDR Setup for Ollama and Gemma 4 26B on a Mac mini (99 pts)

arXiv AI

  • ActionParty: Multi-Subject Action Binding in Generative Video Games
  • Steerable Visual Representations
  • Grounded Token Initialization for New Vocabulary in LMs for Generative Recommendation

Nature Chemistry

    Nature Biotechnology

      Apr 03, 2026

item.title: Daily Digest — March 29, 2026
item.description:
Tech Community Discussions (Hacker News)

  • OpenYak – An open-source Cowork that runs any model and owns your filesystem
    25 points, 6 comments
    Read on Hacker News
  • Founder of GitLab battles cancer by founding companies
    843 points, 190 comments
    Read on Hacker News
  • CSS is DOOMed
    256 points, 57 comments
    Read on Hacker News
  • AI overly affirms users asking for personal advice
    582 points, 444 comments
    Read on Hacker News
  • The case for becoming a manager
    16 points, 3 comments
    Read on Hacker News

Latest AI/ML Research (arXiv)

  • Vega: Learning to Drive with Natural Language Instructions
    Vision-language-action models have reshaped autonomous driving to incorporate languages into the decision-making process. However, most existing pipelines only utilize the language modality for scene ...
    Read on arXiv
  • Drive My Way: Preference Alignment of Vision-Language-Action Model for Personalized Driving
    Human driving behavior is inherently personal, which is shaped by long-term habits and influenced by short-term intentions. Individuals differ in how they accelerate, brake, merge, yield, and overtake...
    Read on arXiv
  • Training the Knowledge Base through Evidence Distillation and Write-Back Enrichment
    The knowledge base in a retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) system is typically assembled once and never revised, even though the facts a query requires are often fragmented across documents and buri...
    Read on arXiv
  • PackForcing: Short Video Training Suffices for Long Video Sampling and Long Context Inference
    Autoregressive video diffusion models have demonstrated remarkable progress, yet they remain bottlenecked by intractable linear KV-cache growth, temporal repetition, and compounding errors during long...
    Read on arXiv
  • PixelSmile: Toward Fine-Grained Facial Expression Editing
    Fine-grained facial expression editing has long been limited by intrinsic semantic overlap. To address this, we construct the Flex Facial Expression (FFE) dataset with continuous affective annotations...
    Read on arXiv

Chemistry Research (Nature Chemistry)

Biology & Biotech (Nature Biotechnology)

High-signal digest from open sources • Sunday, March 29, 2026 at 01:39 AM EDT

Sources: Hacker News • arXiv (AI/ML) • Nature Chemistry • Nature Biotechnology

item.title: Daily Digest — March 29, 2026
item.description:
Tech Community Discussions (Hacker News)

  • OpenYak – An open-source Cowork that runs any model and owns your filesystem
    26 points, 6 comments

  • Founder of GitLab battles cancer by founding companies

  • CSS is DOOMed
    255 points, 57 comments
    Read on Hacker News
  • AI overly affirms users asking for personal advice
    581 points, 443 comments
    Read on Hacker News
  • The case for becoming a manager
    16 points, 3 comments
    Read on Hacker News

Latest AI/ML Research (arXiv)

  • Vega: Learning to Drive with Natural Language Instructions
    Vision-language-action models have reshaped autonomous driving to incorporate languages into the decision-making process. However, most existing pipelines only utilize the language modality for scene ...
    Read on arXiv
  • Drive My Way: Preference Alignment of Vision-Language-Action Model for Personalized Driving
    Human driving behavior is inherently personal, which is shaped by long-term habits and influenced by short-term intentions. Individuals differ in how they accelerate, brake, merge, yield, and overtake...
    Read on arXiv
  • Training the Knowledge Base through Evidence Distillation and Write-Back Enrichment
    The knowledge base in a retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) system is typically assembled once and never revised, even though the facts a query requires are often fragmented across documents and buri...
    Read on arXiv
  • PackForcing: Short Video Training Suffices for Long Video Sampling and Long Context Inference
    Autoregressive video diffusion models have demonstrated remarkable progress, yet they remain bottlenecked by intractable linear KV-cache growth, temporal repetition, and compounding errors during long...
    Read on arXiv
  • PixelSmile: Toward Fine-Grained Facial Expression Editing
    Fine-grained facial expression editing has long been limited by intrinsic semantic overlap. To address this, we construct the Flex Facial Expression (FFE) dataset with continuous affective annotations...
    Read on arXiv

Chemistry & Biology

Coming soon - working on stable data sources for chemistry and biology papers.

High-signal digest from open sources • Sunday, March 29, 2026 at 01:37 AM EDT

Sources: Hacker News • arXiv (AI/ML)

Latest AI/ML Research (arXiv)

  • Vega: Learning to Drive with Natural Language Instructions
    Vision-language-action models have reshaped autonomous driving to incorporate languages into the decision-making process. However, most existing pipelines only utilize the language modality for scene ...
    Read on arXiv
  • Drive My Way: Preference Alignment of Vision-Language-Action Model for Personalized Driving
    Human driving behavior is inherently personal, which is shaped by long-term habits and influenced by short-term intentions. Individuals differ in how they accelerate, brake, merge, yield, and overtake...
    Read on arXiv
  • Training the Knowledge Base through Evidence Distillation and Write-Back Enrichment
    The knowledge base in a retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) system is typically assembled once and never revised, even though the facts a query requires are often fragmented across documents and buri...
    Read on arXiv
  • PackForcing: Short Video Training Suffices for Long Video Sampling and Long Context Inference
    Autoregressive video diffusion models have demonstrated remarkable progress, yet they remain bottlenecked by intractable linear KV-cache growth, temporal repetition, and compounding errors during long...
    Read on arXiv
  • PixelSmile: Toward Fine-Grained Facial Expression Editing
    Fine-grained facial expression editing has long been limited by intrinsic semantic overlap. To address this, we construct the Flex Facial Expression (FFE) dataset with continuous affective annotations...
    Read on arXiv

Biology & Biotech (arXiv q-bio)
